description: A child-friendly compact binocular with 8x magnification. Lightweight and easy to use, ideal for young birdwatchers. Available in soft yellow and light green.
An entry-level full-size binocular from the RSPB own brand range. Lightweight and straightforward to use.
The 8x42 Skylark offers a bright, wide view in a full-size body. A solid entry-level choice for birdwatching.
The 10x42 Skylark gives extra reach for open habitat birding at a very accessible price point.
A compact, lightweight binocular ideal for travel. Features ED glass for improved clarity. Waterproof and pocket-sized.
The 10x version of the Avocet Compact. Extra magnification in a pocketable body with ED glass optics.
A mid-size binocular delivering a good balance of portability and optical performance. Popular choice for day trips.
Full-size 8x42 in the Avocet range. A reliable, well-rounded binocular at an accessible price.
The 10x42 Avocet suits open-country birding where extra reach is useful. Full-size, waterproof body.
A wide-field 8x40 binocular from RSPB. Good light gathering and a spacious field of view.
The Harrier range launched in 2018 with dielectric and phase-coated BaK-4 prisms for improved brightness and colour accuracy. Close focus to 2m. Fully waterproof.
The 8x42 Harrier brings dielectric prism coatings and a 2m close focus to a full-size waterproof body. Sharp imagery with high contrast and accurate colours.
The 10x42 Harrier offers extra power for scanning open habitats, with the same dielectric prism tech and 2m close focus as the 8x models.

The Harrier X is a step up from the standard Harrier, adding Super High Reflective (SHR) dielectric prism coatings and ED glass to eliminate colour fringing. Ultra-close focus. Fully waterproof with a compact magnesium-alloy body.
The 8x42 Harrier X combines SHR dielectric prisms with ED glass and an ultra-close focus in a shock-resistant rubber-armoured body.
The 10x42 Harrier X offers extra magnification with all the SHR dielectric and ED glass technology of the X range.

The Buzzard is a mid-priced wide-angle binocular with ED and field-flattening lenses, phase-coated dielectric prisms, and a very close focus of around 1.3m in practice. Weighs 740g. A sharp, bright performer with excellent low-light capability.
The 10x42 Buzzard brings all the ED and field-flattening optics of the Buzzard range with extra magnification for open habitat use.

The RSPB flagship binocular. Made in Japan with a magnesium body, Phase and SHR-coated Schmidt-Pechan prisms, and flat-field multicoated ED lenses. Exceptional low-light performance. 140m/1000m field of view, 16.7mm eye relief, close focus to 2m (1.44m measured). Comes with 10-year warranty.
The 10x42 version of the RSPB flagship Sparrowhawk. Same Japanese optics, magnesium body, ED and SHR prism technology as the 8.5x model — with extra magnification. Close focus to 1.5m.
A compact, lightweight monocular for quick views on the go. Rubber armoured and easy to use one-handed.
The 10x25 Avocet monocular offers extra magnification in a pocketable single-tube design.
A full-size 8x42 monocular. Useful for travelling light without sacrificing aperture.
The 10x42 version of the Avocet full-size monocular. Good reach and light gathering in a single-tube format.
A pocket monocular with HD optics. Compact and lightweight — ideal for travelling.
A truly portable scope kit. No tripod required — a flexible, compact option for beginners or on-the-move birding. Features quality optics at an accessible price.
A mid-range scope kit with a 65mm ED objective for sharp, bright views. Comes complete with eyepiece and case.
The 80mm ED Harrier scope kit brings improved light-gathering over the 65mm. Excellent for longer range viewing. Includes eyepiece and case.
The RSPB Buzzard 80mm is the flagship own-brand scope. High-quality ED optics in a robust body, supplied with case.
A compact, lightweight Viking scope at an entry-level price. Suitable for beginners or as a travel scope.
The ED version of the Viking Swallow adds extra-low dispersion glass for noticeably cleaner colour and sharper images.
The 80mm Swallow is Viking's full-sized spotting scope, supplied with a 20-60x zoom eyepiece and carry case. A versatile scope for reserve use.

Designed by Marc Newson, the CL Curio is the smallest, lightest binocular in the Swarovski range. Ultra-compact and foldable, yet delivering 90% light transmission and a characteristically flat, sharp Swarovski image. Available in black and burnt orange.

Compact, folding binocular with full Swarovski optical quality. Aluminium housing with a dual-hinge bridge folds remarkably small. SWAROBRIGHT, SWAROTOP and SWARODUR coatings throughout. 90% light transmission. Close focus 2.5m. Available in the Wild Nature version (green).
The 10x version of the CL Pocket. Extra magnification in the same folding aluminium body with full Swarovski coatings. Close focus 2.5m.

A compact, lightweight binocular that delivers genuine Swarovski quality in a mid-size body. 132m/1000m field of view, 16mm eye relief, 2.7m close focus. Weighs 477g. Fully waterproof. Available in green, anthracite and other colours. Comes with Urban Jungle or Wild Nature field bag.
The 10x30 Companion gives extra reach in the same compact body as the 8x. Field of view 108m/1000m, 16mm eye relief, 2.7m close focus. 485g.

The EL Swarovision with field flattener lenses and HD fluoride glass has been the benchmark birding binocular for over a decade. SWAROVISION technology gives edge-to-edge clarity with no rolling ball effect. 20mm eye relief, 1.5m close focus. Open-bridge design.
The 10x42 EL Swarovision. Same HD fluoride glass, SWAROVISION field flatteners and 20mm eye relief as the 8.5x, with 10x magnification. Close focus 1.5m. Weight 840g.

The smallest NL Pure, delivering the widest FOV in the 32mm class. 8x magnification with Swarowski's SWAROVISION technology and the distinctive ergonomic NL body design. Available in green or burnt orange.
The 10x32 NL Pure in the distinctive ergonomic body. Wide-angle FOV with 10x magnification. Available in green or burnt orange.

The 8x42 NL Pure has the widest field of view of any 42mm binocular — 477ft at 1000 yards (145m/1000m). The revolutionary ergonomic body with NL's distinctive wasp-waist profile. SWAROVISION field flatteners give a virtually distortion-free image. 91% light transmission. 18mm eye relief. Close focus 2m. 840g. Available in green or burnt orange.
The 10x42 NL Pure. Field of view of 399ft at 1000 yards — wider than many 8x42 binoculars. SWAROVISION, 18mm eye relief, close focus 2m. 850g. Available in green or burnt orange.
The 12x42 NL Pure brings high-power observation in the NL ergonomic body. Widest field of view for a 12x binocular. 91% light transmission. 850g.
The 10x52 NL Pure delivers exceptional brightness in low light thanks to its large 52mm objective. Designed for demanding observation conditions. Available in green or burnt orange.
The most powerful NL Pure. 14x magnification with a 52mm objective for serious long-range work. Comes with a tripod adapter stud recommended.

Zeiss SFL stands for SmartFocus Lightweight. The 8x30 is a premium compact that weighs just 460g but delivers a 142m/1000m field of view and close focus of 1.5m. UHD optical concept with T* and LotuTec coatings. Magnesium body. Field flattener lenses for edge-to-edge sharpness. Made in Japan.
The 10x30 SFL delivers excellent magnification at just 460g. FOV 120m/1000m, close focus 1.5m, 18mm eye relief. UHD concept with T* and LotuTec. Magnesium body.

The 8x40 SFL is shorter than most 42mm binoculars yet has a 5mm exit pupil. FOV 140m/1000m. UHD glass, T* and LotuTec coatings, magnesium body. 640g. Widely regarded as one of the best mid-size birding binoculars available.
The 10x40 SFL — same body and weight as the 8x40, with 10x magnification. FOV 115m/1000m, 4mm exit pupil. UHD glass, T* and LotuTec coatings.

The classic Leica pocket binocular. Foldable aluminium chassis with HDC multi-coating. Splash resistant (not fully waterproof). Field of view 113m/1000m, close focus 3m, weight 235g. An elegant companion for travel, theatre or sightseeing.

specs.Coating: HDC multicoating, P40 phase, HLS prism

description: The 10x25 BCA Trinovid. HDC and AquaDura coated, P40 phase-corrected BaK-4 prisms. FOV 95m/1000m, close focus 5m, weight 255g. Foldable double-hinge design. Splash resistant.

specs.Coating: HDC multicoating, P40 phase, HLS prism

description: A mid-size Leica with an exceptionally short close focus of just 1m (3.3ft). FOV 115.5m/1000m, 17mm eye relief, weight 650g. Phase-corrected BaK-4 prisms with HighLux (HLS) mirror coatings. Nitrogen-filled, waterproof. Made in Portugal.

specs.Coating: HDC fully multi-coated, P40 phase, HLS prism

description: A full-size Leica Trinovid with 17mm eye relief and 1.8m close focus (approx 1.5m measured). FOV 124m/1000m, weight 750g. Phase-corrected BaK-4 prisms with HighLux mirror coatings. Nitrogen-filled, waterproof. Made in Portugal.

specs.Coating: HDC multicoating, P40 phase, HLS prism

description: The 10x42 Trinovid HD. FOV 103m/1000m, 15mm eye relief, close focus 1.6m, weight 730g. Phase-corrected BaK-4 prisms with HLS coatings. Nitrogen-filled, waterproof. Made in Portugal.

specs.Coating: HDC multicoating, P40 phase, HLS prism

description: The Kite APC 12x42 uses gimbal-based image stabilisation with a 2° correction angle — far beyond conventional IS binoculars. Fully waterproof. 17mm eye relief. Battery life 120 hours. Perfect for open country, coastal birding, and marine use where steadiness at high power is essential.

The most powerful APC binocular. 16x magnification with 2° gimbal-based image stabilisation. Ideal for scanning at long range — seawatching, estuary birding, or anywhere a scope and tripod aren't practical. Fully waterproof. Note: 14mm eye relief is shorter than the 12x model.
A lightweight compact binocular from Nikon. Roof prism design, multi-coated lenses, twist-up eyecups. Suited to general use and travel.
The 10x Travelite EX. Extra magnification in the same compact multi-coated body. Twist-up eyecups.
A premium mid-size Nikon with ED glass, dielectric multi-layer prism coatings and a 1.5m close focus. Lightweight, nitrogen-filled and waterproof. A highly regarded birding binocular.

Swarovski's compact spotting scope with a fixed-body design. The 17-40x zoom eyepiece provides an enormous field of view compared to traditional compact scopes. 186ft–102ft at 1000 yards. 20mm eye relief. Weight 895g. Ideal for travel.

The Swarovski ATX angled body without objective module — purchased separately to mix and match with 65, 85 or 95mm objectives. The modular ATX/STX system allows one eyepiece to be shared across objective sizes.
A straight-view Swarovski scope with 65mm HD objective, supplied with 25-50x wide-angle eyepiece and stay-on case. A premium full-size scope for serious observation.
The 80mm HD ATS scope with 25-50x eyepiece and stay-on case. Swarovski's flagship straight-view scope — exceptional resolution and brightness.
The 65mm objective module for the ATX/STX system, paired with a 25-60x eyepiece. Wide, bright views for general birding. Lighter than the 85 and 95mm options.
The 85mm ATX objective with 25-60x eyepiece. A step up in light-gathering from the 65mm — noticeably brighter in poor light or at maximum magnification.
The flagship 95mm ATX objective. Maximum light-gathering for challenging viewing conditions. Paired with 30-70x eyepiece — the most powerful optic in the Swarovski scope range.

Kowa's compact flagship spotting scope. The TSN-55A (angled) uses a 55mm pure fluorite crystal objective paired with the full-size TE-11WZ II wide-angle zoom eyepiece — giving 192ft–105ft FOV at 1000 yards. Dual fast/fine focus knobs. Fully waterproof (IPX7). Made in Japan. 34oz. Close focus 9.2ft.

The Kowa TSN-500 is an ultra-compact 50mm spotting scope — light enough to carry anywhere. The fixed 20-40x zoom eyepiece and close focus of 2.5m make it ideal for beginners, casual birders, or anyone who wants a capable scope without the bulk. Polycarbonate body, nitrogen-filled and waterproof to IPX6. Available in angled (TSN-501) and straight (TSN-502) versions.
